name: Generate Tests by Recording Browser Actions
description: Use Playwright Codegen to record user interactions in the browser and automatically generate test scripts with proper locators.
trendingReason: Codegen dramatically lowers the barrier to writing E2E tests for non-testing-experts

steps.title: Launch the code generator
steps.description: Open a browser with the codegen recorder attached.
steps.tip: Interact with the page normally. Every click, type, and navigation is recorded as test code in real time.

steps.title: Generate code for a specific viewport
steps.description: Record tests at a specific screen size for responsive testing.

steps.title: Record with authentication state
steps.description: Start codegen with a saved authentication state to skip login flows.
steps.tip: First save auth state with 'npx playwright codegen --save-storage=auth.json' after logging in manually.

steps.title: Use the locator picker
steps.description: Launch the inspector to find robust locators for page elements.

steps.title: Generate tests in a specific language
steps.description: Output test code in your preferred language.
steps.tip: Supported targets include playwright-test (TypeScript), python-pytest, java-junit, and csharp-mstest.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Playwright Codegen Workflow
set -euo pipefail

TARGET_URL=${1:-"http://localhost:3000"}

echo "=== Playwright Codegen ==="
echo "Target: $TARGET_URL"
echo ""
echo "Instructions:"
echo "1. A browser window will open with the code generator"
echo "2. Interact with the page to record actions"
echo "3. Copy the generated code from the right panel"
echo "4. Paste into your test file"
echo ""
echo "=== Tips ==="
echo "- Click 'Record' to start/stop recording"
echo "- Use 'Pick locator' to find the best selector for an element"
echo "- Use 'Assert visibility' to add expect() assertions"
echo "- Use 'Assert text' to verify text content"
echo ""
echo "=== Launching Codegen ==="
npx playwright codegen \
  --target=playwright-test \
  "$TARGET_URL"

faq.question: How reliable are codegen-generated locators?
faq.answer: Playwright codegen uses a smart locator strategy that prefers role-based and text-based locators (getByRole, getByText, getByLabel) over CSS selectors. These are more resilient to DOM changes. Always review and refine generated locators for production tests.

faq.question: Can I record assertions, not just actions?
faq.answer: Yes. In the codegen toolbar, click 'Assert visibility' to assert an element is visible, or 'Assert text' to verify text content. These generate expect() calls in the output code.

faq.question: How do I handle dynamic content during recording?
faq.answer: Codegen records what you interact with. For dynamic elements, record the interaction and then manually edit the test to add appropriate waitFor conditions or use Playwright's auto-waiting which handles most cases automatically.
